articles.title: DGFT Clarifies UIN Generation Timeline for Export Credit Interest Subvention Claims Under EPM Scheme
articles.summary: The Directorate General of Foreign Trade (DGFT) clarified that banks can submit interest subvention claims under the Export Promotion Measures (EPM) scheme even if Unique Identification Numbers (UINs) were generated after the export credit was disbursed. This relaxation was introduced to address challenges faced by exporters and lenders during the transition phase of the scheme. The decision aims to ease compliance for stakeholders involved in export credit interest subvention claims.
articles.raw_summary: DGFT clarified that banks may submit interest subvention claims even where UINs were generated after export credit disbursal during the transition phase. The relaxation was introduced to address practical implementation challenges faced by exporters and lending institutions under the EPM scheme. The post DGFT Clarifies UIN Generation Timeline for Export Credit Interest Subvention Claims Under EPM Scheme appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: DSIR Approval Binding: ITAT Allows Section 35(2AB) Deduction for R&D Expenses
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that once the Department of Scientific and Industrial Research (DSIR) certifies R&D expenditure under Section 35(2AB), the tax department cannot reject the claim without following proper legal procedures. This decision strengthens the binding nature of DSIR certification in disputes over weighted deductions for research and development expenses. The ruling impacts how companies claim tax benefits for R&D investments in India.
articles.raw_summary: Tribunal ruled that once DSIR certifies R D expenditure under Section 35(2AB), the Assessing Officer cannot disregard the claim without following the statutory procedure. The decision reinforces the importance of DSIR certification in weighted deduction disputes. The post DSIR Approval Binding: ITAT Allows Section 35(2AB) Deduction for R D Expenses appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.summary: West Bengal Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari announced two key decisions: lifting the previous government's ban on potato sales to other states and granting the CBI permission to prosecute officials in multiple scams. The lifting of the potato sale ban reverses a trade restriction policy, while the CBI sanction enables legal action against officials involved in alleged financial misconduct. These actions reflect the state government's policy shifts and enforcement priorities in governance and law

Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari alleged that the previous government had withheld permission to protect 'corrupt bureaucrats.' This decision enables the CBI to investigate and prosecute alleged corruption within the state's bureaucracy.

This ruling highlights the procedural aspects of tax reassessment and the consequences of non-compliance.

articles.title: Vijay oath-taking confusion: Governor’s role, floor test rules and past Supreme Court judgment
articles.summary: The article examines the role of the Governor in appointing a Chief Minister when a single party has an absolute majority in the Assembly, citing the Sarkaria Commission Report (1983). It highlights the constitutional and procedural rules governing such situations, including the requirement for a floor test. The discussion is relevant to India's governance framework and may influence future political appointments and legislative procedures.
articles.raw_summary: As per the Sarkaria Commission Report (1983), which discusses the role of the governor, if a single party has an absolute majority in the Assembly, the leader of that party should automatically be asked to become the Chief Minister.

articles.title: The toll of structural adjustments on the global South and a case for accountability
articles.summary: In the 1980s, the IMF and World Bank imposed conditions on loans to countries in the global South, including many in Africa and Latin America. Decades later, these countries continue to face weak public health systems and high poverty levels. A new paper argues that these institutions owe reparations for the damage caused by these policies.
articles.raw_summary: In the 1980s, IMF and World Bank put conditions on financing the global South. Decades later, many countries in the regions struggle with weak public health systems and high levels of poverty. The institutions owe reparations for the damage caused, a new paper argues.

articles.title: GST AAR Allows ITC on Inputs & Services Used for Setting Up CCV Tower for Cable Manufacturing
articles.summary: The Gujarat Appellate Authority for Advance Ruling (AAR) ruled that input tax credit (ITC) is available on goods and services used to construct a CCV Tower for cable manufacturing. The ruling clarified that such structures qualify as 'plant and machinery' under Section 17(5) of the CGST Act, enabling businesses to claim tax credits for essential infrastructure. The decision impacts tax compliance and manufacturing costs for industries relying on such support systems.
articles.raw_summary: Gujarat AAR held that input tax credit on goods and services used for constructing a CCV Tower was admissible as the structure formed an essential structural support for manufacturing machinery. The ruling clarified that such support systems are covered within “plant and machinery” under Section 17(5) of the CGST Act. The post GST AAR Allows ITC on Inputs   Services Used for Setting Up CCV Tower for Cable Manufacturing appeared first on TaxGuru .

Sarma emphasized that Assam has long demanded sealed borders but noted that meaningful closure requires coordinated action from neighboring states like West Bengal. The move highlights inter-state cooperation on national security and border management.
articles.raw_summary: Mr. Sarma said that many organisations in Assam demand that the border must be sealed in the State, but this was meaningless without a similar closure in West Bengal

articles.title: Black Mineral Water Taxable at 5% in Absence of Sugar & Flavouring
articles.summary: The Gujarat Appellate Authority for Advance Ruling (AAR) ruled that black mineral water containing added minerals but no sugar, sweeteners, or flavouring is taxable at 5% GST under HSN 22011010. The decision applies Entry 146 of Notification No. 09/2025. This clarifies the tax treatment for such products in the absence of specific exemptions.
articles.raw_summary: The Gujarat AAR held that black mineral water containing added minerals but no sugar, sweeteners, or flavouring is classifiable under HSN 22011010. articles.title: Customs Duty on Gold & Silver Raised to 15% (Effective May 13, 2026)
articles.summary: The Indian government has raised the customs duty on gold and silver bullion to 15%, effective from May 13, 2026. The change is implemented through revised Basic Customs Duty (BCD), Social Welfare Surcharge (SWS), and Agriculture Infrastructure Development Cess (AIDC) structures. This policy aims to standardize import duties for these precious metals. The decision impacts importers, traders, and the broader precious metals market in India.
articles.raw_summary: The new customs notifications standardize the effective import duty on gold and silver bullion at 15% through revised BCD, SWS, and AIDC structures. The post Customs Duty on Gold   Silver Raised to 15% (Effective May 13, 2026) appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Less than 1% public vehicles have trackers, panic buttons. SC says no fitness certificate without them
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has ruled that public vehicles must have location tracking devices and emergency panic buttons installed to receive a fitness certificate. This requirement was mandated under the Motor Vehicles Act in 2018, following the Nirbhaya case. Currently, less than 1% of public vehicles comply with this rule. The court emphasized that no fitness certificate will be issued without these safety measures.
articles.raw_summary: Location tracking devices and emergency buttons had been made mandatory under Motor Vehicles Act in 2018, two years after Nirbhaya case.

This decision clears a legal obstacle for Earth Infrastructures Ltd to revive its stalled projects and provides relief to thousands of homebuyers awaiting possession of their homes. The ruling underscores accountability in real estate regulation and could influence future policy on housing delays and developer penalties.
articles.raw_summary: SC ruling is significant as it clears legal hurdle in reviving Earth Infrastructures Ltd projects and offers relief to thousands of homebuyers waiting for possession of their homes.

The tribunal ruled that reassessment proceedings become void if sanction is taken from the wrong authority, setting a precedent for tax procedural compliance. articles.raw_summary: The Pune ITAT held that reassessment proceedings become void when approval under Section 151 is taken from the wrong authority. articles.title: Right to Education in Mother Tongue Flows from Article 19(1)(a); Supreme Court Directs Rajasthan to Introduce Rajasthani in Schools
articles.summary: The Supreme Court directed the Rajasthan government to introduce Rajasthani language in schools, emphasizing the right to education in one's mother tongue as a fundamental right flowing from Article 19(1)(a). The Court highlighted that understanding in one's own language is essential for meaningful participation in society. The directive follows a recognition of the existential importance of education in the mother tongue for comprehension and societal engagement.
articles.raw_summary: The ability to understand and be understood in one s own language is not a matter of convenience, but a matter of existential rights, for comprehension must necessarily precede meaningful participation in the society and day to day life activities. This change aims to reduce manual errors and improve efficiency in GST refund claims.

articles.title: ITAT Restricts Section 14A Disallowance as It Cannot Exceed Exempt Income
articles.summary: The Lucknow Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that disallowances under Section 14A of the Income Tax Act, read with Rule 8D, cannot exceed the exempt dividend income earned by the taxpayer. The tribunal restricted the addition to the actual amount of exempt income, setting a precedent for tax assessments. This decision impacts how tax authorities calculate disallowances for expenses incurred to earn exempt income.

The ITAT directed fresh verification of land records to resolve the dispute. This ruling impacts tax policy enforcement related to agricultural land exemptions.

SC reiterates IBC can’t be used as tool for coercion, debt recovery in breach of contract cases
articles.summary: The Supreme Court of India reiterated that the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC) cannot be used as a tool for coercion or debt recovery in cases that are primarily contractual disputes. The court upheld a ruling by the National Company Law Appellate Tribunal (NCLAT), emphasizing that insolvency proceedings should not be invoked for breaches of contract. This decision reinforces the original intent of the IBC to address genuine insolvency situations rather than commercial disagreements.
articles.raw_summary: SC said insolvency proceedings can't be invoked for debt recovery in disputes that are predominantly contractual in nature. In doing so, it upheld an NCLAT ruling.

articles.title: No Fishing Enquiries in Reassessment – Pune ITAT Quashes 148 Proceedings After AO Dropped Original Issue!
articles.summary: The Pune Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) quashed reassessment proceedings under Section 148 of the Income Tax Act after the Assessing Officer (AO) dropped the original issue. The ITAT ruled that the AO cannot continue reassessment on a new issue without issuing a fresh notice under Section 148. This decision impacts tax reassessment procedures and prevents 'fishing enquiries' by tax authorities. The ruling follows the AO's acceptance of the taxpayer's explanation for cash deposits.
articles.raw_summary: Pune ITAT held that once the Assessing Officer accepted the explanation for cash deposits, reassessment could not continue on a completely new issue without issuing a fresh notice under Section 148. articles.title: CPC Cannot Deny 25% Tax Rate Benefit Due to Missing ITR Column: ITAT Pune
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Pune ruled that the Centralized Processing Centre (CPC) cannot deny a 25% tax rate benefit to taxpayers solely because the turnover column was missing in their income tax return (ITR) form. The tribunal stated that the CPC's application of a 30% tax rate due to this omission was not justified under the law. This decision reinforces the legal provision for a concessional tax rate and clarifies procedural enforcement by tax authorities.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal ruled that CPC’s application of a 30% tax rate merely due to absence of turnover disclosure in the return form could not override the concessional rate provided by law. The post CPC Cannot Deny 25% Tax Rate Benefit Due to Missing ITR Column: ITAT Pune appeared first on TaxGuru .

This decision impacts Flipkart's tax liability and sets a precedent for e-commerce logistics taxation in India.

articles.title: TSMC says global chip market to hit $1.5 trillion by 2030 as AI drives growth
articles.summary: TSMC, a leading semiconductor manufacturer, forecasts the global chip market will exceed $1.5 trillion by 2030, driven by AI and high-performance computing. The company is expanding advanced chip production in Arizona, Japan, and Germany to meet rising demand. This growth highlights the strategic importance of semiconductors, which could influence India's technology and industrial policies.

The company is expanding its advanced chip production and packaging capacity. New facilities are being built in Arizona, Japan, and Germany to meet future demand.

articles.title: India losing investor attention to Asia’s AI-electronics surge, says DBS economist
articles.summary: Global capital is shifting towards electronics and AI in Asia. India's foreign direct investment slowdown is not a major concern. Asia's export boom offers opportunities. India should integrate more into supply chains and adopt pragmatic approaches to Chinese investment. Realistic energy price adjustments and targeted support for vulnerable groups are also recommended.

articles.title: Market Stress: How the Middle East war is rattling global markets and economies
articles.summary: The ongoing conflict in the Middle East is driving up global oil prices and weakening currencies, particularly affecting Asia due to its reliance on imported energy. Rising food inflation and consumer costs are adding to economic pressures worldwide. Investors are closely monitoring how central banks, including India's, may respond to these geopolitical and financial challenges.
articles.raw_summary: The Middle East conflict is significantly impacting global economies, driving up oil prices and weakening currencies. Asia, heavily reliant on imported energy, faces particular vulnerability, with rising food inflation and consumer costs adding to economic pressures worldwide. Investors are closely watching central bank responses to these escalating geopolitical and financial challenges.

Developers are planning significant new supply, anticipating sustained growth and a substantial investment opportunity in the retail real estate sector.

The ruling impacts hiring practices for government teaching posts and reinforces constitutional protections against arbitrary employment decisions.

What the mission does not address may be more revealing than what it does.

articles.title: 89 killed as thunderstorm batters Uttar Pradesh
articles.summary: A powerful thunderstorm with lightning and strong winds struck Uttar Pradesh, killing 89 people, injuring 53, and causing widespread destruction of homes and property. The storms also killed 114 animals. Collapsing walls and falling trees were the primary causes of fatalities. The incident highlights the need for stronger disaster preparedness and infrastructure safety measures.
articles.raw_summary: Powerful thunderstorms, lightning, and strong winds have claimed at least 89 lives across Uttar Pradesh. The devastating storms, which lasted for hours, also injured 53 people and killed 114 animals. Widespread destruction of homes and property was reported, with collapsing walls and falling trees being the primary causes of fatalities.

articles.title: Medical Prescription For Abdominal Pain Insufficient To Prove Mother Is Alcoholic: Allahabad High Court Grants Custody Of Child

articles.title: 'Hostile Witness Testimony Can Be Used To Acquit Also, Not Just Convict' : Supreme Court Sets Aside Murder Conviction
articles.summary: The Supreme Court on May 13 acquitted a man accused of murdering a person from a Scheduled Caste/Scheduled Tribe (SC/ST) category. The Court ruled that hostile witness testimony can be used not only to convict but also to acquit if it discredits the prosecution's case. The prosecution's witnesses failed to support the prosecution's version of the crime's location, undermining the entire case.
articles.raw_summary: Observing that the evidence of hostile witnesses can be used not only for conviction but also to discredit the prosecution case and support acquittal, the Supreme Court on Wednesday (May 13) has acquitted a man accused of killing a man belonging to a SC/ST category. articles.title: AAC Bricks Not Classifiable as Ceramic Products Due to Low Firing Temperature: Gujarat AAR
articles.summary: The Gujarat Appellate Authority for Advance Ruling (AAR) ruled that Autoclaved Aerated Concrete (AAC) bricks cannot be classified as ceramic products because their autoclaving process does not meet the firing temperature requirement for ceramics. Instead, the AAR classified AAC bricks under cement/concrete products (Heading 6810). This decision impacts tax classification and compliance for manufacturers and suppliers of AAC bricks in Gujarat.
articles.raw_summary: The Gujarat AAR held that AAC bricks cannot be classified under Chapter 69 as ceramic products because the autoclaving process does not satisfy the firing requirement prescribed for ceramic goods. The ruling classified AAC bricks under Heading 6810 as articles of cement or concrete. The post AAC Bricks Not Classifiable as Ceramic Products Due to Low Firing Temperature: Gujarat AAR appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: GSTAT Holds Builder Guilty of Profiteering for Failing to Pass Full ITC Benefit to Homebuyers
articles.summary: The Goods and Services Tax Appellate Tribunal (GSTAT) ruled that a builder engaged in profiteering by not passing the full Input Tax Credit (ITC) benefit to all homebuyers in a housing project. The tribunal upheld findings that excess benefits given to some buyers could not offset shortfalls owed to others. The developer had accepted the Directorate General of Anti-Profiteering (DGAP) report, which led to the ruling.
articles.raw_summary: GSTAT upheld anti-profiteering findings after the developer accepted the DGAP report concerning ITC benefits in a housing project. The Tribunal ruled that excess benefit given to some buyers cannot offset shortfall payable to others. The post GSTAT Holds Builder Guilty of Profiteering for Failing to Pass Full ITC Benefit to Homebuyers appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: The Gold Duty Shock: A Symptom, Not the Disease
articles.summary: The article argues that the sharp increase in gold import duty was triggered by pressure on India’s forex reserves, rising oil prices, and widening dollar outflows. It claims the move may revive smuggling, hurt the jewellery sector, and expose deeper weaknesses in economic management. The post The Gold Duty Shock: A Symptom, Not the Disease appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.summary: The Indian government has increased the Minimum Support Price (MSP) for paddy by ₹72 per quintal for the 2026-27 kharif marketing season. articles.title: Cabinet raises Minimum Support Prices for 14 kharif crops
articles.summary: The Union Cabinet approved increased Minimum Support Prices (MSP) for 14 kharif crops for the 2026-27 marketing season. The MSP for all notified kharif crops has been set at least 50% above production costs, with sunflower seed seeing the largest absolute increase. Cotton and other oilseeds also received substantial boosts. The MSP for common paddy has been fixed at Rs 2,441 per quintal.
articles.raw_summary: The Union Cabinet has approved significant hikes in Minimum Support Prices for 14 Kharif crops for the 2026-27 marketing season. Sunflower Seed sees the largest absolute increase. Cotton and other oilseeds also receive substantial boosts. MSPs for all notified Kharif crops are set at least 50% above production costs. Common paddy MSP is fixed at Rs 2,441 per quintal.

articles.title: Centre raises MSP for 14 kharif crops, boosts support for pulses and oilseeds
articles.summary: The Indian government has increased the Minimum Support Price (MSP) for 14 kharif crops, with the highest hike of 61% for moong. The MSP for paddy (rice) has been raised by Rs 72 per quintal to Rs 2,441 for the common variety. This decision aims to support farmers, particularly for pulses and oilseeds, and is based on expected margins over production costs.
articles.raw_summary: Based on expected margins over cost of production, the MSP hike is the most for moong at 61%, followed by bajra and maize (56% each) and tur (54%). For other crops, it is estimated to be around 50%. The MSP for paddy (rice), the main kharif crop, has been increased by Rs 72 per quintal, taking it to Rs 2,441 for the 'common' variety and Rs 2,461 for 'Grade A'.

articles.title: Kharif reset: Farmers may ditch paddy and maize for soybean, pulses amid monsoon fears
articles.summary: As the kharif season approaches, Indian farmers are considering growing soybean and pulses like urad and masur instead of paddy and maize. This shift is driven by concerns over erratic monsoons and potential fertilizer shortages. Maize planting is expected to decline as farmers opt for lower-risk, less fertilizer-dependent crops amid unpredictable weather conditions.
articles.raw_summary: As the kharif season approaches, farmers are leaning towards cultivating oilseeds like soybean and pulses such as urad and masur, primarily due to worries about erratic monsoon patterns and looming fertilizer shortages. Following a recent surge, maize planting is forecasted to decline, as farmers shift towards crops that are less fertilizer-dependent and lower in risk amid unpredictable weather conditions.

articles.title: China’s factory inflation surges to a near 4-year high; domestic demand remains tepid
articles.summary: China's factory prices rose by 2.8% in April, the fastest increase since July 2022, ending a period of deflation. The surge is linked to disruptions in energy supplies due to the Iran war, but weak domestic demand and a sluggish labor market are limiting firms' ability to raise prices. The situation highlights broader economic challenges in China that could affect global trade and energy markets.

articles.title: Gold, Silver imports to cost more after customs duty, cess hike: Details here
articles.summary: The Indian government has increased the customs duty and cess on gold and silver imports from 6% to 10%, and imposed a 10% duty on platinum and related precious metal products. This decision will raise the cost of importing these metals. The move is part of a fiscal policy adjustment by the government.
articles.raw_summary: With this increase, the total duty on gold and silver now stands at 10% from 6% earlier. Platinum, and related precious metal products, including hooks, clasps, clamps, pins and screw backs used in manufacturing, will also have a 10% duty.

articles.title: For Agra’s footwear artisans, the west Asia war is more than just sand in the shoe
articles.summary: The ongoing war in West Asia is disrupting Agra’s footwear industry, where artisans and exporters face rising costs and delayed supplies. Orders are harder to fulfill on time, squeezing profit margins and threatening recovery even if global tensions ease. The industry warns it may take at least a season to bounce back. Workers and businesses are bearing the brunt of these supply chain disruptions.

With margins thinning and orders getting harder to execute on time, the industry says it may take at least a season to recover even if global tensions ease.

articles.title: Heavy rain and dust storm leave 33 dead across Uttar Pradesh

Rescue operations were disrupted by the extreme weather conditions.

Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath has ordered immediate relief and compensation for affected families, with district authorities tasked to expedite assistance. Surveys are underway to assess damage to crops, livestock, and homes, as rescue efforts faced disruptions due to the severe weather.

articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that visiting a temple or performing rituals is not mandatory for a Hindu to prove their belief. The court emphasized that personal faith and belief, rather than outward practices, define religious identity. This judgment reinforces constitutional protections for religious freedom under Articles 25-28 of the Indian Constitution.

articles.title: ‘Wayanad will be next Amethi’: Posters warning Rahul, Priyanka Gandhi surface in Kerala as Venugopal leads CM race
articles.summary: Posters comparing Wayanad to Amethi were placed outside the district Congress office in Wayanad and Vadra’s MP office in Kozhikode, Kerala. The posters reference Rahul and Priyanka Gandhi, suggesting political rivalry in the region. The message, attributed to Congress leader Venugopal, hints at a potential leadership contest in Wayanad. This reflects local political tensions that may shape future electoral or policy priorities.
articles.raw_summary: The posters appeared outside the district Congress committee office in Wayanad and Vadra’s MP office in Mukkam in Kozhikode

articles.title: CBSE class 12 pass percentage falls by 3.19% this year? Experts list reasons behind marginal fall
articles.summary: The CBSE Class 12 pass percentage dropped to 85.2% this year from 88.39% last year. Experts have cited reasons for the marginal decline. The Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) is the regulatory body overseeing this examination system. The fall may prompt further analysis or potential policy adjustments in India's education sector.
articles.raw_summary: The CBSE class 12 pass percentage stood at 85.2%, a decline from last year’s 88.39%.

articles.title: Anxiety to ‘finally’: The frustrating wait for CBSE 12th results ends for students
articles.summary: The Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) declared the Class 12 results for 2026 on May 13, ending a two-day wait for students. The results were initially announced as 'coming soon' on DigiLocker on May 11 before being officially released around 1:30 PM on May 13. This marks the completion of the evaluation process for CBSE-affiliated schools.
articles.raw_summary: DigiLocker first shared a 'coming soon' update for the CBSE 12th results 2026 on May 11, and the results were actually declared on May 13, around 1:30pm.

articles.title: Chaos, criticism, walkout: Vijay's trust vote win in Tamil Nadu did not lack drama
articles.summary: The Tamil Nadu assembly witnessed chaos, infighting, and a walkout during a trust vote involving Vijay. The event reflects political tensions within the state government. Such procedural disputes can impact legislative functioning and governance stability.
articles.raw_summary: Vijay's trust vote today did not lack any drama, much like Vijay's own movies, as the assembly saw chaos, infighting, and also a walkout.

articles.title: CBSE 12th result 2026 out declared: How to download scorecards on DigiLocker, UMANG app
articles.summary: The Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) has declared the Class 12 results for 2026, which are available for download on platforms like DigiLocker and the UMANG app, in addition to the official CBSE websites. This move aims to streamline result distribution using government digital infrastructure. The CBSE is a statutory body under the Ministry of Education, Government of India.
articles.raw_summary: Apart from the main website, the Class 12 results are also available on platforms such as cbse.gov.in, cbseresults.nic.in, digilocker.gov.in, and results.gov.in

articles.title: Tamil Nadu: AIADMK split out in open as 25 MLAs back TVK during floor test
articles.summary: The AIADMK party in Tamil Nadu split openly as 25 of its MLAs supported TVK during a floor test in the assembly. TVK secured the majority with 144 MLAs backing it, including the 25 AIADMK legislators aligned with CV Shanmugam and SP Velumani. This political development highlights internal party dynamics and potential shifts in governance.
articles.raw_summary: TVK formally proved its majority in the assembly on Wednesday with the support of 144 MLAs, including backing from 25 AIADMK legislators aligned with CV Shanmugam and SP Velumani.

articles.title: Existing health insurance cover is inadequate? Top-up vs Super Top-up – Which one should you take?
articles.summary: The article explains the difference between top-up and super top-up health insurance plans in India, which allow individuals to increase their coverage without a significant rise in premiums. It addresses concerns about the inadequacy of existing health insurance policies and suggests these options as solutions. The discussion is relevant to India's healthcare and financial sectors, particularly in the context of rising medical costs and insurance accessibility.
articles.raw_summary: With a top-up or super top-up plan, an individual can significantly increase their health insurance cover without a substantial increase in premium amount.

articles.title: Centre raises gold, silver import duties to 15% amid West Asia turmoil
articles.summary: The Indian government has raised import duties on gold and silver from 6% to 15%, and on platinum from 6.4% to 15.4%. This decision, announced amid turmoil in West Asia, aims to regulate precious metal imports. The move impacts trade dynamics and revenue for the government.
articles.raw_summary: Import duties on gold and silver has been increased from 6% to 15%, while the import duty on platinum has been increased from 6.4% to 15.4%.

articles.summary: Scientists working on a Hantavirus vaccine reported that after successful animal trials, they were ready to collaborate with international partners for human trials. However, the project stalled due to a lack of funding. The team expressed disappointment, stating, 'We were close, then ran out of money.'

articles.summary: The article examines China's increasing military assertiveness and economic self-sufficiency, which are hardening its stance on key issues. The focus is on China's growing confidence and its potential impact on international relations.
articles.raw_summary: China is increasingly self-sufficient, militarily assertive and economically insulated—and it is digging in on core issues of contention.

articles.title: Russia’s Sarmat missile: All about nuclear-capable ballistic missile, dubbed as world’s ‘most powerful’
articles.summary: Russia announced its Sarmat missile, a nuclear-capable ballistic missile, during a televised meeting with military officials. This development is relevant to India as it may influence regional security and defence policies, given India's strategic partnerships and geopolitical considerations.
articles.raw_summary: The announcement, made during a televised meeting with top military officials, marked Russia’s latest attempt to showcase its next-generation strategic weapons.

articles.title: Reading the tea leaves on the Xi-Trump meet
articles.summary: The article analyzes the upcoming meeting between U.S. President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping, noting that the summit is unlikely to resolve the ongoing adversarial relationship between the two countries. Both nations are expected to continue their long-term strategic competition, which could have implications for global trade, technology, and security. The analysis suggests that frictions between the U.S. and China will persist despite the high-level talks.
articles.raw_summary: The Trump–Xi summit is unlikely to reset the two countries’ adversarial relationship; frictions will persist as both sides prepare for long-term competition

articles.title: CM Vijay’s oath ceremony sparks CPI, DMK outrage over Thamizhthai Vaazhthu: What is the controversy about?
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu's new Chief Minister Vijay was sworn in, promising a secular government. However, the CPI and DMK criticized the order of the state song 'Tamil Thaai Vaazhthu' during the ceremony, demanding it be prioritized in all future government events. The controversy centers on the song's placement and its symbolic importance in state functions. The opposition parties argue this reflects a deviation from secular governance.
articles.raw_summary: Vijay was sworn in as Tamil Nadu's Chief Minister, promising a ‘new era’ of secularism. However, CPI and DMK criticised the order of state song Tamil Thaai Vaazhthu during the ceremony, calling for it to be prioritised in future government events.

articles.title: India's gold imports: How it impacts our economy | An explainer
articles.summary: India's gold imports are at an all-time high, reaching USD 71.98 billion in 2025-26. This surge is driven by increased prices and trade agreements. High imports pressure India's trade deficit and foreign exchange. The government is reviewing trade policies to curb these imports and protect reserves.
